burdenMetrics.label: Domestic generation benchmark
burdenMetrics.note: UNITAR and ITU place Nigeria third in Africa for domestic e-waste generation.

burdenMetrics.label: RMRDC total burden claim
burdenMetrics.note: The higher estimate points to a much larger total burden once imports are counted.

burdenMetrics.label: Used electronics entering Lagos
burdenMetrics.note: Large import flows keep Nigeria at the center of the second-hand electronics trade.

burdenMetrics.label: Used EEE through Lagos ports
burdenMetrics.note: Port inflows add directly to the country's repair, reuse, and disposal pressure.

dependencyStats.label: Data usage growth
dependencyStats.note: Monthly internet data usage rose by 168% between January 2023 and December 2025, increasing device turnover and future waste volume.

dependencyStats.label: Informal processing share
dependencyStats.note: More than 90% of Nigeria's e-waste is absorbed by informal recovery because formal recycling infrastructure remains marginal.
